channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19740] tunefs.lustre must protect against specifying multiple devices
item.description:
tunefs.lustre is designed to configure a single Lustre device at a time.
However, the command accepts multiple block devices as arguments, and when this happens, it writes incorrect metadata to the devices, resulting in mixed-up mountdata files of the OST/MDT targets

For example

Let's assume MDT devices:
/dev/nvme0n1 -> testfs-MDT0000
/dev/nvme1n1 -> testfs-MDT0001

# tunefs.lustre /dev/nvme0n1 
checking for existing Lustre data: found

   Read previous values:
Target:     testfs-MDT0000
Index:      0
Lustre FS:  testfs
Mount type: ldiskfs
Flags:      0x1065
              (MDT MGS first_time update no_primnode )
Persistent mount opts: user_xattr,errors=remount-ro
Parameters: mgsnode=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12 failover.node=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12


   Permanent disk data:
Target:     testfs:MDT0000
Index:      0
Lustre FS:  testfs
Mount type: ldiskfs
Flags:      0x1065
              (MDT MGS first_time update no_primnode )
Persistent mount opts: user_xattr,errors=remount-ro
Parameters: mgsnode=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12 failover.node=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12

The following command can be accepted and, it reads mountdata from /dev/nvme1n1 (MDT1) and write it into /dev/nvme0n1 (MDT0)

# tunefs.lustre /dev/nvme0n1 /dev/nvme1n1

This behavior is dangerous and can silently corrupt mountdata file of OST/MDT targets.

# tunefs.lustre /dev/nvme0n1 
checking for existing Lustre data: found

   Read previous values:
Target:     testfs-MDT0000
Index:      1
Lustre FS:  testfs
Mount type: ldiskfs
Flags:      0x1061
              (MDT first_time update no_primnode )
Persistent mount opts: user_xattr,errors=remount-ro
Parameters: mgsnode=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12 failover.node=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12


   Permanent disk data:
Target:     testfs:MDT0001
Index:      1
Lustre FS:  testfs
Mount type: ldiskfs
Flags:      0x1061
              (MDT first_time update no_primnode )
Persistent mount opts: user_xattr,errors=remount-ro
Parameters: mgsnode=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12 failover.node=10.0.211.1@o2ib12:10.0.211.2@o2ib12

Writing CONFIGS/mountdata

This must be prevented, and an error should be returned instead.
item.summary: tunefs.lustre must protect against specifying multiple devices

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Xiao Yang <xyang@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64352
Subject: LU-19740 utils: Avoid redundant rewrite for tunefs.lustre
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 1d737f7992949ba8212e0c64af85f2a9053afca4

"Xiao Yang <xyang@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64324
Subject: LU-19740 utils: Improve tunefs.lustre and mkfs.lustre
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ffc3323a37893541aa7962edaf496a23f74a1c0f
Hi Yang Sheng, Xiao is currently working on several improvements and will push the patch soon. I would appreciate it if you could review his patch when he submits it.
Hi, Ihara, I don't mind you touch the patch as you like. Or you can work out it as a new patch. This way can unify the different opinion more quickly.

I am not saying --writeconf does not change mountdata. But --writeconf is required only when the user wants to apply changes to the configuration logs, which I think is fine.

The Only problem is that tunefs.lustre will update mountdata if someone invoke it without any options.

I agree we could internally switch to 'dry run' mode when tunefs.lustre is invoked without any option, no matter the device is mounted or not.

Hi, Sebastian, The --writeconf is not only applied to config log. It also modify the mountdata. In fact, The Only problem is that tunefs.lustre will update mountdata if someone invoke it without any options. If we call it with a option, then it means we want to do a certain action. It is not problem. But it is really wired to update mountdata without any option. This is only problem i think.

Even without a --dry-run option, I would expect tunefs.lustre <dev> to run in dry-run (read-only) mode by default.
Thoughts?

Note that --writeconf and --dryrun are taking effect on different aspects. While --dryrun is global and prevents any write modifications, --writeconf only applies to the configuration logs, and not to mountdata. Because the --dryrun option exists, it is implicit that without it, the device would be modified. So what we are going to do is to prevent modifications on a mounted device unless a new option is given on the command line to force the modification action. But we are not going to make tunefs.lustre "read-only" by default in the general case.
Hi, Ihara, Yes, This is really a dangerous behaviors. I think we should run it in dryrun mode as default. Just write out things when explicit parameter was given.

I’m also surprised that tunefs.lustre, even without --writeconf, runs in overwrite (read-write) mode by default. Why does it need to read and write mountdata? Is this expected behavior?
Even without a --dry-run option, I would expect tunefs.lustre <dev> to run in dry-run (read-only) mode by default.
Thoughts?

Hi, Sebastien, I have noticed it. The tune.lustre assumes last one parameter as device name while read ldd, but update device as result of parse_args(). They are inconsistent when invoked with multiple device names. I'll fix it with mounting check patch.

Thanks for filling this ticket. According to the man page, tunefs.lustre should only accept one device, so the following should be forbidden:

# tunefs.lustre /dev/nvme0n1 /dev/nvme1n1

ys could you please handle this?
